summary.authoritative_rule: reentry-to-add handoff stays blocked after lockout not because raw dates disappear but because post-lockout board-state derivation has not yet produced any surface on which unlock context could be judged

triage_rows.rationale: post-lockout local intraday dates do exist, so the vacancy is not a raw-data absence
every post-lockout raw trading date still lacks the derived board-state surface needed to evaluate unlock context
without post-lockout derived board coverage there can be no lawful unlock-positive or unlock-worthy context
handoff remains blocked because the bridge has no post-lockout board surface to reopen into
this vacancy audit clarifies a missing context layer and does not promote execution

interpretation: V1.34GY converts the vacancy audit into the current governance verdict for the shadow replay lane.
The next blocker is now narrower than before: post-lockout local dates exist, but the board-level derived surface has not been extended beyond lockout end, so unlock consultation cannot yet restart.
